seoTitle: Windsor Gardens vs Winkie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Windsor Gardens and Winkie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Windsor Gardens vs Winkie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Windsor Gardens and Winkie.
heading: Windsor Gardens vs Winkie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Windsor Gardens, South Australia and Winkie, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Windsor Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Winkie (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Windsor Gardens or Winkie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Windsor Gardens has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Winkie scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Windsor Gardens scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Winkie scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Windsor Gardens or Winkie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Windsor Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
